role_title: Product Manager, Self-Serve Business Growth Lead

level_signals: Help build and mentor a growing team of PMs as the self-serve growth function scales
Own self-serve business growth goals, metrics, and executive-level narratives

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have led product-led growth initiatives for B2B, SaaS, developer, or business-facing products.
Have experience building growth loops, activation flows, team adoption experiences, monetization paths, or expansion motions.
Have worked on team-based, workspace, collaboration, developer, or multi-user products.
Are comfortable owning business outcomes even when execution depends on multiple teams and product surfaces.
Are highly analytical and can translate customer behavior, product data, and business goals into a focused product strategy.
Are strong at cross-functional influence, strategic alignment, and executive communication.
Have operated at significant scale, such as large user bases, meaningful revenue responsibility, or high-growth product lines.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in PLG, B2B SaaS, self-serve growth, monetization, lifecycle growth, or developer products.
Experience with team-based, workspace, collaboration, or developer-facing products.
Experience partnering with Sales, Revenue, or Go-To-Market teams on handoff, upsell, or expansion motions.

scope.surface: Self-serve business growth for OpenAI products (Codex, OpenAI API) across full customer journey: acquisition, conversion, activation, retention, expansion, monetization
scope.business_model: B2B self-serve / product-led growth, developer-facing, with Enterprise upsell motion
primary_metrics_inferred: Usage growth across self-serve business products
core_responsibilities: Define the end-to-end product strategy for self-serve business growth across acquisition, conversion, activation, retention, expansion, and monetization
Identify and prioritize the highest-impact product opportunities that improve team adoption, usage, retention, and expansion
Develop growth motions that help teams building with OpenAI get to value faster
Partner with core product teams to shape roadmap priorities
Own self-serve business growth goals, metrics, and executive-level narratives
Work closely with Sales and Go-To-Market teams to design handoff, upsell, and expansion motions
Use customer insights, product analytics, experimentation, and business data to inform strategy
Help build and mentor a growing team of PMs

green_flags: Clear ownership scope covering the full growth funnel (acquisition through expansion)
Explicit people leadership component — building and mentoring a PM team
Strong cross-functional mandate with named partners (Sales, GTM, Data Science, etc.)
Directly tied to high-profile OpenAI products (Codex, OpenAI API)
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated — unclear if role requires relocation
Seniority level implied ('senior product leader') but not explicitly defined

Duplicate qualification bullets appear in the JD (PLG/B2B SaaS and workspace/developer experience listed twice under both 'you might thrive' and standalone bullet points), suggesting possible editorial inconsistency

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'senior product leader' language, executive communication ownership, and people management signal)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'Help build and mentor a growing team of PMs')
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and self-serve/enterprise context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.type (not stated in JD; marked as unclear)
ambiguities: The 'You might thrive in this role if you:' section contains both unconditional bullets (treated as hard requirements) and items that appear to be soft preferences (labeled 'Experience in PLG...' and 'Experience with team-based...' and 'Experience partnering with Sales...'), creating ambiguity. The latter three were classified as nice_to_haves because they appear as standalone sub-bullets without the same 'Have' framing as the main bullets.
No location, remote policy, or visa sponsorship information is stated anywhere in the JD.
